content.full_text:
June 16, 1976


Mr. Frederick Bold, Jr.
Bold and Polisner
1990 North California Blvd.
Walnut Creek, California 94596

Re: Your Letter of May 27, 1976

Dear Mr. Bold:

Your letter presents two issues; first, because of pending
litigation, there currently exist no opinions or drafts dealing
with §87207(b) regarding disclosure of clients or customers.

Proposed Opinion No. 75-120 is not applicable in this area.
Proposed Opinion No. 75-119 is; however, because of the pending
litigation, no opinion or draft exists at the present time.

Your second question appears to refer to what your clients
are required to disclose pursuant to §87207(b). I assume, there-
fore, that the clients to which you refer are 87200 filers.

§87207(b) disclosures are only required when income must be
disclosed. Further, §87207(b) must be read in conjunction with
§82030(a) to determine whether or not the income of a business
entity is required to be reported under §87207(b) when the offi-
cial owns a ten percent interest or greater in the business entity
(see Government Code §82030(a)) and the filer's pro rata share
of fees from each client was equal to or greater than $1,000.

Similarly, under §87207(b)(3), the filer must disclose the
gross receipts from each client, providing he owns a ten percent
interest or greater, and his pro rata share of gross receipts
from each client is $10,000 or greater during the calendar year.

If you need further assistance, please contact Alfredo
Flores, Conflicts of Interest Division, (916) 322-6444.

Sincerely,

Aldredo Flores
Staff Attorney
Conflicts of Interest
Division

AF/gb
bee:Jeannie

[The text in this image appears to be printed upside down and is largely illegible in the provided scan. The document appears to be a typed letter or official document on yellowed paper with two holes at the top for fastening. There is a handwritten notation "A-44-41" in the upper left corner and what appears to be a date "1938" or similar in the lower right corner. The body text is too faint and inverted to accurately transcribe without significant guessing at the content.]

FREDERICK BOLD AND POLISNER
JEFFREY D. POLISNER

BOLD AND POLISNER
ATTORNEYS AT LAW
SIXTH FLOOR
1890 NORTH CALIFORNIA BOULEVARD
WALNUT CREEK, CALIFORNIA 94596

AREA CODE 415
TELEPHONE 933-7777

May 27, 1976

Fair Political Practices Commission
926 J Street, Suite 522
Sacramento, California 95814

Gentlemen:

Will you kindly send me a copy of any opinion
(adopted or in draft) that deals with the requirements
under Section 87207(b) for directors of agencies to
disclose their clients (if lawyers) or their customers
(if retail merchants).

I have advised the directors of the agencies I
represent that they need not disclose the identity of
a client or customer unless the client or customer has
had, or is likely to have, a business relationship with
the agency or otherwise might foreseeably affect
materially the decisions made or participated by the
director.

Is this subject covered by proposed decision
75-119 or 75-120?

Thank you for any assistance you can give me in
this matter.

Yours very truly,

Frederick Bold, Jr.

FB:bm
